journal_entry.description: Pledge of $1,000,000 donation to humanitarian food aid program in Somalia via 1-month promissory note
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, a donation pledge creates an immediate expense (Charitable Donations Expense) when the commitment is unconditional and legally binding. The issuance of a promissory note establishes a formal liability (Notes Payable) for the amount pledged. This is a non-exchange transaction where the entity receives no goods or services in return. The expense is recognized when the pledge is made, not when cash is paid. No tax implications as charitable donations are not subject to HST/GST.

output.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note. The donation represents an expense increase (charitable contribution/donation expense). The promissory note is a liability that will be credited, not debited. Therefore, on the debit side we have 1 expense increase.

debit_classifier.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note. The donation represents an expense increase (charitable contribution/donation expense). The promissory note is a liability that will be credited, not debited. Therefore, on the debit side we have 1 expense increase.

output.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note. The credit side records: (1) a liability increase for the promissory note payable ($1,000,000), and (2) an expense decrease is NOT recorded because donations are expenses that are being recognized (debited), not reversed. The credit is only the liability created by the promissory note.

credit_classifier.reason: Target pledged a donation and issued a promissory note. The credit side records: (1) a liability increase for the promissory note payable ($1,000,000), and (2) an expense decrease is NOT recorded because donations are expenses that are being recognized (debited), not reversed. The credit is only the liability created by the promissory note.
